Patch Set 4: Verified+1

The patch resolves the performance degradation. Here is test resutls.

128 clients, 1024 processes, 4 servers, 4 x MDT, 8 x OST
mdtest  -F -u -n 2000 -N 1 -i 3 -p 10 -d /testfs/<DIR> -v

File Creation Rate
master(commit: ) /wo OST pool        : 365298.700
master(commit: ) /w  OST pool        : 200025.444
master(commit: ) + patch /wo OST pool: 376404.132
master(commit: ) + patch /w  OST pool: 398395.886

Patrick, it looks like the read lock is fine - Shuichi tested and this patch is enough to get the OST Pools performance back.

Can you please review again.

parents.subject: LU-19113 llite: cfs_delete_from_page_cache() keep page locked

commit.subject: LU-19106 lod: check QoS data in pool before down_write
commit.message:
LU-19106 lod: check QoS data in pool before down_write

Just like ltd_qos_is_usable() does, define pool_qos_is_usable()
to check whether QoS data in pool is up-to-date and balanced before
expensive qos write lock is taken.

Fixes: e642e75cde02 ("LU-13363 lod: do object allocation in OST pool")
Test-Parameters: ostcount=8 testlist=conf-sanity env=ONLY=133
Signed-off-by: Emoly Liu <emoly@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I9d17108b649ba5689f02d5f5eee098d030db3d5b
